dependencies: Dependence on popular artists and their willingness to tour
Limited number of artists that can headline major tours or sell out larger venues
Reliance on third parties to create and perform live entertainment events
Dependence on venue availability and lease renewals
Reliance on third-party ticketing clients for Ticketing segment revenue
risks: Inability to secure popular artists could limit revenue generation
Competition for key employees with artist relationships
Venue lease renewals may not be on acceptable terms
Third-party venue operators may compete with owned venues
Dependence on unrelated parties to create live music events

regulatory_risks: Privacy laws and protection of personal or sensitive information (GDPR, California Consumer Protection Act)
United States Foreign Corrupt Practices Act compliance
United Kingdom Bribery Act 2010 compliance
Primary ticketing and ticket resale services regulations
Construction, renovation and operation of venues regulations
Licensing, permitting and zoning, including noise ordinances
Human health, safety, security and sanitation requirements
Service of food and alcoholic beverages regulations (dram shop statutes)
Americans with Disabilities Act (ADA) and UK Disability Discrimination Act (DDA) compliance
Hazardous and non-hazardous waste and environmental protection laws
Sales and other taxes and withholding of taxes
Marketing activities via telephone and online regulations
Historic landmark rules
Antitrust laws and competition regulations
Consumer protection and unfair business practice laws
Amended Final Judgment restrictions from DOJ (through end of 2025)
State and federal investigations into ticketing practices
Potential liability for entertainment taxes and incidents at events
Zoning and noise level restrictions affecting venue operations
Department of Justice antitrust complaint filed May 2024 seeking divestiture of Ticketmaster

sanction_risks: United States Department of the Treasury's Office of Foreign Assets Control sanctions on certain Russian individuals and entities
European regulators sanctions on certain Russian individuals and entities
Sanctions elsewhere in the world could restrict or limit business activities
